description: Exercises multiple concurrent browser surfaces (multi-surface Phase 4, ADR 01019) end-to-end: goTo as the only session opener (engine keyword auto-open and { browser, name } named-session open), selecting sessions by engine keyword and by name, re-selecting an existing session instead of opening a duplicate, active-follows-focus across sessions (surface-less steps act on the most recently focused session), windows/tabs INSIDE a named session (the Phase 3 selectors compose under Phase 4 sessions), `surface` on type/find/runBrowserScript/screenshot across sessions, whole-browser closeSurface (bare name, engine keyword, and { browser, name } forms) with focus fallback to the most recently focused survivor, and idempotent closes of already-closed and never-opened surfaces. Tests run in the default headless-firefox context; each test gets its own session set, torn down by the context sweep.

tests.description: Two concurrent sessions of the SAME engine: the default firefox surface (auto-named "firefox") plus a named second firefox session ("admin"), addressed by name, with tabs inside the named session, cross-session steps, and whole-browser closes.

steps.description: goTo with { browser, name } opens a SECOND firefox session and navigates it
The named session is now the active surface (surface-less step)
The engine keyword addresses the default session (exact-name match beats the engine fallback)
Select the named session again by name
goTo re-selects the existing named session (no duplicate) and opens a tab INSIDE it
Phase 3 tab selectors compose with Phase 4 session names
type into an element of the named session's tab, waiting on a find condition
runBrowserScript runs in the named session
screenshot captures the DEFAULT session while the named one is active
Tab addressing in the default session still works with two sessions open
Close the whole named session by its BARE NAME (identity-only string)
Active falls back to the most recently focused survivor (the default session)
Re-closing the closed session is an idempotent PASS no-op
Closing a never-opened surface is an idempotent PASS no-op (array form)

tests.description: Two engines at once: the default firefox surface plus a chrome session auto-opened by goTo's engine keyword; cross-engine addressing, typing, and whole-browser close by engine keyword with focus fallback.

steps.description: goTo with a bare engine keyword AUTO-OPENS the second browser
The new chrome session is the active surface
Hop back to firefox by engine keyword
type into the chrome session from a firefox-active state (object form)
screenshot captures the chrome session
goTo re-selects the running chrome session (no duplicate open)
Close the whole chrome browser by engine keyword
Active falls back to the surviving firefox session
Re-closing chrome is an idempotent PASS no-op
